name: Verse Markers

releases.author: gabrielrenderos
releases.description:
Maintenance release. No user-facing feature changes — markers, references, ranges, disjoint ranges, hover previews, navigation, and flash highlighting all behave the same.

### Improved
- **Popout window support:** DOM lookups, element creation, and animation timing use the active window's document, so previews, navigation, and the flash highlight work correctly in popped-out windows.
- **Internal cleanup:** timer calls now use `window.requestAnimationFrame`, aligning with Obsidian's plugin guidelines.

### Docs
- README now documents installing from the Obsidian community store.
releases.name: 1.0.2 — Popout windows & timer hygiene

releases.author: gabrielrenderos
releases.description:
Maintenance release addressing Obsidian community-review feedback. No user-facing feature changes — verse markers, references, ranges, disjoint ranges, hover previews, navigation, and flash highlighting all behave the same.

### Fixed
- **iOS / iPadOS compatibility:** removed a regex lookbehind that isn't supported on WebKit before 16.4, so verse markers now detect correctly on older mobile devices. Matching behavior is unchanged on every other platform.

### Improved
- **Popout window support:** DOM lookups, element creation, and animation timing now use `activeDocument` / `activeWindow`, so previews, navigation, and highlighting work in popped-out windows.
- **API hygiene:** switched to the non-deprecated `MarkdownRenderer.render`, moved inline styles into the stylesheet, use `Setting().setHeading()` for the settings section, and tidied async/typing per Obsidian's plugin guidelines.

### Notes
- Settings tab: the top section is now labeled "Reference syntax" (cosmetic).
releases.name: 1.0.1 — Mobile/iOS compatibility & review fixes

releases.author: gabrielrenderos
releases.description:
### Features
- Inline verse numbering with `[N]` markers in reading view, styled with your theme accent color.
- Cross-note verse references via `[[File#verse-N]]` links with rich hover previews.
- Range references (`[[File#verse-4:6]]`) and disjoint ranges (`[[File#verse-4:6/8:10]]`) that skip verses.
- Heading-split, footnote-split, and authored (`[5a]`) verse parts.
- Click-to-navigate with smooth scrolling and temporary flash highlighting.
- "Copy verse reference" commands and a URI protocol handler.

Licensed under GPL-3.0-or-later.
releases.name: Initial release of Verse Markers.
